channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 How Russia exploits drone incursions in the Baltics—and how to respond | Atlantic Council
item.description: A series of recent drone overflights in Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia, and Finland have revealed a shifting strategy from Russia. The post How Russia exploits drone incursions in the Baltics—and how to respond appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 UAE presidential advisor Anwar Gargash on the Iran war, the Abraham Accords, and Europe-Gulf cooperation | Atlantic Council
item.description: On the sidelines of the Europe Gulf Forum, Gargash shares his thoughts on the Iran war and its far-reaching consequences. The post UAE presidential advisor Anwar Gargash on the Iran war, the Abraham Accords, and Europe-Gulf cooperation appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Pakistan’s High-Wire Act Gets Riskier | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country has sent troops and arms to Saudi Arabia at a delicate moment in the Iran war.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy Working On Teaming SEALs In Mini-Submarines With Underwater Drones | The War Zone
item.description: Pairing uncrewed underwater vehicles and SEALs in submersibles opens the door to new operational possibilities, but there are challenges. The post Navy Working On Teaming SEALs In Mini-Submarines With Underwater Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 After the Castro indictment, what is Trump’s endgame in Cuba? | Atlantic Council
item.description: The pressure campaign raises a number of urgent questions. We turned to experts in the Atlantic Council’s network for answers. The post After the Castro indictment, what is Trump’s endgame in Cuba? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How to build a northern Australia economic hybrid zone | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Northern Australia is often described as constrained by distance, workforce and cost. That framing misses the point. By any other definition, it’s a greenfield industrial system, with abundant energy, world-class mineral endowment, available land and ...

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine and Russia Are Souring on U.S. Negotiations | Foreign Policy
item.description: The two nations, seemingly frustrated by the United States as a mediator, are sizing up alternatives.

item.title: 📍 DAF study finds new space launch site ‘probably’ required | Breaking Defense
item.description: Lt. Gen. David Miller, Space Force deputy for Strategy, Plans, Programs and Requirements, said on Tuesday that the service alone is looking at some 1,000 launches between fiscal 2027 and 2031.

item.title: 📍 Mass Protests Plunge Bolivia Into Political Upheaval | Foreign Policy
item.description: The United States has called the weekslong crisis an “ongoing coup d’état.”

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon CTO wants to give vendors ‘fast’ decisions on buying tech | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The way I’ve approached this — knowing how smaller companies work — is fast yeses and fast nos. The worst thing for a small company is to be dragged through a multi year process,” Pentagon CTO Emil Michael said.

item.title: 🕵️ Quick Take: Progress Continues at New Yalu River Bridge Crossing | 38 North
item.description: Commercial satellite imagery indicates work to build a customs and immigration center on the ... The post Quick Take: Progress Continues at New Yalu River Bridge Crossing appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: ⚛️ Congressional funding concerns about a new nuclear cruise missile | Breaking Defense
item.description: Why one key lawmaker says he’s ‘troubled’ by Energy Department plans to produce a special warhead on schedule.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s War Is Wrecking Trump’s Economy | Foreign Policy
item.description: Inflation is rising and fault lines are widening on disruptions from history’s biggest energy shock.

item.title: 🔐 Naval Special Warfare Command combatant craft set to receive upgrades | Breaking Defense
item.description: The upgrades are expected include the addition of an open system architecture to facilitate the “integration and aggregation of sensors,” PEO-Maritime Capt. Jared Wyrick said.

item.title: 🛡️ The global push for local-currency cross-border payments is intensifying | Atlantic Council
item.description: More than one hundred countries now operate instant payment systems, and growing efforts to link them are reshaping how cross-border transactions get settled in local currencies. The post The global push for local-currency cross-border payments is intensifying appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 Germany Unveils Latest Tranche 4 Eurofighter | The War Zone
item.description: With an advanced radar and other upgrades, the Tranche 4 Eurofighter is increasingly central to Berlin’s airpower plans. The post Germany Unveils Latest Tranche 4 Eurofighter appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Mali Strikes Rebel-Held Kidal as Insurgency Worsens | Foreign Policy
item.description: How long can the country—and the Sahel’s other junta-led governments—hold on?

item.title: 🌍 The UAE’s Syrian Gambit | Foreign Policy
item.description: To thwart competitors and secure new transport corridors, Abu Dhabi has set its sights on Damascus.

item.title: 🏴 A Check-In On North Korea and Russia’s Wartime Partnership | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2025, Choong-Koo Lee wrote, “Putting the Screws on the Partnership Between North Korea and Russia,” where he argued that the United States and South Korea should work together to weaken Pyongyang and Moscow’s military relationship. A year later, we asked him to revisit his…

item.title: 📍 Why humans are staying in the loop with loyal wingman drones | Breaking Defense
item.description: In the second part of a new video series focused on manned-unmanned teaming, the panelists talk about the benefits, and risks, of relying on unmanned systems.

item.title: 📍 A demand-driven energy transition | Atlantic Council
item.description: As electricity becomes a prerequisite for economic growth, the energy transition will be shaped less by targets and more by demand. The post A demand-driven energy transition appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Responsibility is the surest path to resilience in the Minerals Age | Atlantic Council
item.description: As governments race to secure minerals to meet growing energy demand, responsible mining is emerging as a practical path forward to build resilient, enduring supply chains. The post Responsibility is the surest path to resilience in the Minerals Age appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏛️ These American Destroyers Are Equipped With Laser Weapons | The War Zone
item.description: The U.S. Navy has placed directed energy systems on nine surface combatants and is looking to expand testing and deployment of similar systems across the fleet. The post These American Destroyers Are Equipped With Laser Weapons appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Hunt For C-146 Wolfhound Special Ops Transport Plane Replacement Underway | The War Zone
item.description: On any given day, C-146s are criss-crossing the globe to far-flung locales, but they are increasingly difficult to sustain. The post Hunt For C-146 Wolfhound Special Ops Transport Plane Replacement Underway appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Lithuanian lawmakers shelter, Vilnius air traffic suspended due to drone incursion | Defense News
item.description: A drone violated Lithuanian airspace on Wednesday, the latest in a series of security incidents in the Baltic region.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🛡️ Southern border is a ‘sandbox’ for counter-drone tech: NORTHCOM commander | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Guillot said that through JIATF 401, the US military is inviting industry partners to the border to show off their tech aimed at taking down adversary drones.

item.title: 🏴 Germany touts pan-German space command amid European push to supplant US tech | Defense News
item.description: Austria’s Defense Minister Claudia Tanner reaffirmed that Austria plans to put three operationally designated military satellites into orbit next year.

item.title: 🏛️ Airbus to open new A330 tanker facility in Spain, announces demo with Spanish Navy | Breaking Defense
item.description: Announcement of the conversion facility comes after Italy awarded the manufacturer a contract for the planned procurement of the tankers, valued at €1.4 billion ($1.6 billion).

item.title: 🕵️ Special ops leader says Maduro mission set ‘new standard’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: Special Operations Command head Adm. Frank Bradley used a keynote address at SOF Week to laud the “most sophisticated” joint operation special ops have ever conducted.
